title: sedum pulchellum
intro: Sedum pulchellum, also known as the wild stonecrop or pink sedum, is a low-growing, spreading perennial succulent native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, with the roots and leaves being the most active parts used for medicinal purposes. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including soothing skin irritations, reducing fever, and treating respiratory issues. Sedum pulchellum can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, with some herbalists also using it in salves and ointments to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
